blocks.content: That night he opened his MySpace and sat there for a while.

He looked at his top eight. Eli first, then Daisy — same as always, since the beginning. He looked at Daisy's profile. Ryan was still in her first slot. The song on her profile was something he recognized now, something they'd heard on the radio on a morning walk in March and she'd said she liked it. He'd remembered that. He always remembered things like that.
He clicked over to AIM. Daisy was online. He opened a window and typed: hey. Stared at it. Deleted it.

There was a thing he had been carrying since they were twelve years old — since the field, since the fireflies, since the water tower and the porch and the walk home that took the long way. He had picked it up and put it down and picked it back up and carried it through middle school and the Henderson house and AIM and Brooke and the water tower and Ryan Callaway. He had been carrying it for three years and had never once set it down all the way.
He had probably known for a long time.
He thought about what his mom had said: you might be getting close.
He typed Molly into the search bar. Found it. Put it on his profile. Didn't examine why. Closed the laptop.
